title: Philosophy Foundations
description: This course introduces **the study of fundamental questions about reality, knowledge, morality, reasoning, and human existence**. It provides the conceptual foundation for all later philosophy courses.

title: Logic
description: This course studies **the principles of correct reasoning and argumentation**. It covers both informal and formal logic, providing the tools to evaluate arguments, identify fallacies, and construct rigorous proofs.

title: Epistemology
description: This course studies **the nature, sources, limits, and justification of knowledge**. It explores questions about truth, belief, evidence, skepticism, and how humans acquire reliable knowledge.

title: Metaphysics
description: This course studies **the fundamental nature of reality, existence, and being**. It explores questions about what exists, how things persist through time, the nature of causation, and the structure of reality itself.

title: Ethics
description: This course studies **moral philosophy**, exploring what makes actions right or wrong, what people ought to do, and how ethical principles apply to personal life, society, science, business, and technology.

title: Political Philosophy
description: This course studies **the philosophical foundations of politics, government, law, and society**. It explores questions about justice, liberty, equality, rights, authority, and the ideal political order.

title: Philosophy of Mind
description: This course studies **the nature of the mind, consciousness, and mental states**, exploring how thoughts, emotions, perception, and intelligence relate to the brain, the body, and artificial systems.

title: Philosophy of Science
description: This course studies **the nature of scientific knowledge, scientific reasoning, and the methods used to investigate the natural world**. It explores what distinguishes science from non-science and how scientific knowledge evolves.

title: Philosophy of Language
description: This course studies **the nature of language, meaning, and communication**. It explores how words refer to the world, how meaning is constructed, and how language relates to thought, logic, and reality.

title: Aesthetics
description: This course studies **the philosophy of beauty, art, and aesthetic experience**. It explores what makes something beautiful, what distinguishes art, how creativity works, and how people interpret artistic works.

title: History of Philosophy
description: This course studies **the development of philosophical thought across different civilizations and historical periods**. It examines the major philosophers, schools of thought, and the evolution of philosophical ideas.

title: Contemporary Philosophy
description: This course studies **the major philosophical movements, ideas, and debates from the late 19th century to the present**. It examines how philosophy addresses modern issues in science, technology, society, politics, and human existence.
